rulings.Case Title: SHELDON FONG VS. EAST WEST BANK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring Action To Trial
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 30, 2021 line 1. DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Bring Action To Trial is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: VIRGIL DENISE BRADLEY-TYSON VS. DWIGHT W. DAVENPORT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 30, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT DWIGHT DAVENPORT'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is continued for Plaintiff to submit proposed factual support, instead of conclusions, of either (1) a direct relationship between Davenport and Plaintiff or (2) based on community property rights as referred to in opposition to the motion for sanctions (Opposition to Motion for Sanctions 4:17-25). Plaintiff to clearly indicate if causes of action against Davenport arise out of direct relationship with Davenport and representations made by Davenport to Plaintiff directly or claims are made based on Davenport's relationship with Mr. Tyson as a conduit for Plaintiff re: community property. Factual allegations to include a link between the representations made/facts concealed and known at the time the Deed was drafted, the form of Deed, and the damages sustained by Plaintiff. Plaintiff should also be mindful of the sham pleadings doctrine and all the facts that are currently available in this litigation in making the proposed factual allegations supporting the causes of action against Mr. Davenport. Plaintiff's proposed factual assertions to be provided no later than July 16, 2021. Hearing continued to July 28, 2021. No further argument is permitted. Supplemental filing to be limited to the facts. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
